extraction.one_line_summary: CICCONE attacked from breakaway on Mortirolo descent, held off HIRT in final 500m sprint for stage win while NIBALI's early attack on climb split GC group.
tactical_patterns: NIBALI attacked first on Mortirolo wet roads before others expected, forcing reactions that isolated ROGLIČ without teammates.
CARUSO stayed ahead in breakaway specifically to pace NIBALI later, demonstrating Bahrain pre-planned sacrifice strategy for wet descent.
LÓPEZ initially dropped on Mortirolo start but recovered to gain time, suggesting pacing miscalculation rather than weakness on steep pitches.
CICCONE held wheel through 700m sprint setup then launched late, reversing typical track-style leadout that HIRT attempted.

rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: First to attack GC group on Mortirolo despite wet conditions, finished fourth on stage gaining time on multiple rivals.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Isolated without teammates when NIBALI attacked, lost 1:23 to CARAPAZ group and dropped from second to third overall.
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: Won stage from breakaway sprint and consolidated mountains jersey, showing both climbing endurance and finishing speed.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: Best British performance referenced by commentators, suggests breakthrough climbing form on extreme gradients previously unseen.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: Recovered after being dropped early on Mortirolo to finish with leaders, gained time on rivals showing resilience.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Finished fifth on stage with leaders group, gaining time while conserving energy without attacking.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Defended pink jersey successfully, maintained gap over ROGLIČ who lost time, but did not gain significantly.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: Positioned in breakaway specifically to assist NIBALI's later attack, executed team sacrifice strategy effectively.
